evals.prompt: Run verify.sh (old hardcoded version) and capture JSON output
evals.expected_output: JSON evidence file with 7 checks, all producing results

assertions.name: verify-old-produces-valid-json
assertions.description: Old verify.sh produces valid JSON evidence

evals.prompt: Run verify.sh (new parameterized version) WITHOUT --profile flag and compare to old version
evals.expected_output: JSON evidence identical or equivalent to old version output

assertions.name: verify-new-no-profile-produces-valid-json
assertions.description: New verify.sh without profile produces valid JSON

assertions.name: verify-new-no-profile-same-structure
assertions.description: JSON structure matches old version (same keys, same check names)

assertions.name: verify-new-no-profile-same-verdicts
assertions.description: All 7 check verdicts match old version verdicts

evals.prompt: Run verify.sh (new version) WITH --profile flag pointing to helpdesk stack-profile.json
evals.expected_output: JSON evidence equivalent to old version (since profile describes the same helpdesk stack)

assertions.name: verify-with-profile-produces-valid-json
assertions.description: New verify.sh with profile produces valid JSON

assertions.name: verify-with-profile-same-verdicts
assertions.description: All 7 check verdicts match old version

evals.prompt: Run mechanical-checks.sh (old vs new) with --check all and compare
evals.expected_output: Same check results from both versions

assertions.name: mechanical-same-output
assertions.description: Both versions produce equivalent check results
